task: I’m replacing a dead deadbolt and want to make sure I don’t buy the wrong kind. What should I check on the door and the existing hardware before I head to the store?

turns.agent_response:
Check these before you go:

- **Door thickness**: Measure the door where the lock goes. Most deadbolts fit standard residential doors, but you want the exact thickness.
- **Bore hole size**: Measure the big hole through the door. Many deadbolts use a **2-1/8 inch** bore.
- **Backset**: Measure from the **edge of the door** to the **center of the bore hole**. Common sizes are **2-3/8 inch** and **2-3/4 inch**.
- **Cross bore and edge bore**: Make sure the lock body and latch will fit the holes already drilled.
- **Door swing**: Know whether it’s **left-hand or right-hand**, and whether it swings **in or out**.
- **Existing latch style**: Bring the old latch with you if you can, or at least note the shape and size of the faceplate on the door edge.
- **Strike plate**: Check whether the new deadbolt comes with one, and whether your door frame can accept the same size.
- **Single-cylinder vs double-cylinder**: If the old one uses a key on both sides, make sure you buy the same type unless you want to change it.
- **Finish and trim style**: Not required for fit, but it helps match the rest of the hardware.

Best move: **take the old deadbolt apart and bring the parts to the store** if possible. At minimum, bring:
- the latch
- the thumbturn/inside piece
- the outside keyed piece
- a few photos of the door edge and both sides

If you want, I can give you a **quick measuring checklist** you can use in 5 minutes before you shop.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right stuff. Measure the door thickness, bore hole, backset, and check the latch/strike plate. I’d also pull the old deadbolt apart and take the parts with me — less chance of getting handed the wrong one by somebody who doesn’t care.

Quick checklist:
- door thickness
- big hole size
- backset: edge of door to center of hole
- latch faceplate shape/size
- whether it’s single or double cylinder
- right-hand or left-hand swing
- strike plate size

If the old one is still in one piece, bring it. I don’t trust guessing at the store.
